feat: use official Foundry FoundryCBAgent hosting adapter

Replace raw aiohttp server with azure-ai-agentserver-core's
FoundryCBAgent for production deployment. Falls back to aiohttp
for local dev/tests when the adapter isn't installed.

Key changes:
- serve.py: main() tries FoundryCBAgent first (uvicorn+Starlette),
  falls back to aiohttp if import fails
- serve.py: extracted _parse_input_to_messages() shared between both
  server paths

- deploy.yml: passes AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT in CLI create env vars

Why this should fix the RequestTimedOut:
The FoundryCBAgent adapter uses uvicorn (not aiohttp) and handles
Foundry sidecar integration, health probes, CORS, tracing, and
graceful shutdown — exactly what the Foundry infrastructure expects.
All 345 tests pass (tests use _init_app() directly, unaffected).

commit.message:
fix(ci): feature-gate PyO3 so server binary links on macOS (#44)

* fix(ci): feature-gate PyO3 so server binary links on macOS

On macOS the linker is strict about undefined symbols. PyO3's
extension-module feature emits -undefined dynamic_lookup only for
cdylib targets, not for bin targets, so the server binary link fails
with unresolved Python C-API symbols.

Fix: make pyo3 and numpy optional behind a default-enabled 'python'
Cargo feature. server/Cargo.toml now uses default-features = false,
so the server never pulls in PyO3 and links cleanly on all platforms.

Also:
- Remove unused pyo3-build-config build-dependency (no build.rs)
- Add CI check for server build (cargo check server/Cargo.toml)
- Update release.yml macOS comments to reflect the real fix

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: generate WebP images during blog deploy step (#276) (#281)

The blog's _includes/responsive-image.html emits <source srcset="...webp">
tags for every image. HTML-Proofer validates these at build time, causing
N failures per missing WebP (one per page that references the image via
related-posts or blog listing).

Add a Pillow-based WebP conversion step in the 'Publish to Blog Repository'
job, immediately after copying the PNG assets. Handles both image and chart
PNGs. Gracefully skips if the source PNG is absent and logs a warning
(non-fatal) if Pillow fails.

commit.message:
fix: Azure tool serialization crash causing 100% CLI fallback

tool.parameters is a voluptuous Schema, not a dict. It was being passed
directly to the JSON serializer, causing 'Object of type Schema is not
JSON serializable' on every Azure-routed request. All Azure calls failed
at ~3ms and fell back to CLI.

Fix: use voluptuous_openapi.convert() with the LLM API's custom_serializer
to properly convert tool schemas to JSON-compatible dicts, matching how
HA's built-in conversation integrations (OpenRouter, Google AI, etc.)
handle this.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

EXISTS subquery always returns false. Using a SECURITY DEFINER
function bypasses RLS, matching the pattern of all other device RPCs.

Add spectral analysis module (Phase 3.3)

Implement nova.spectral with six categories of advanced spectral tools:

1. Wavelength calibration: line identification, polynomial solution
   fitting, and one-shot calibration pipeline
2. Sky subtraction: 1-D and 2-D sky removal (median and polynomial)
3. Radial velocity: FFT cross-correlation with sub-km/s accuracy,
   Doppler shifting, barycentric correction
4. Spectral classification: index measurement (9 standard indices),
   equivalent width, template matching
5. Emission line fitting: Gaussian and pseudo-Voigt profiles, single
   and multi-line simultaneous fitting via scipy.optimize
6. Multi-order echelle: order tracing, extraction, and SNR-weighted
   overlap merging

Uses lazy scipy imports with graceful fallback, numpy-style docstrings,
and pure ASCII throughout. All functions are fully implemented and tested.
